---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Experimental design under the baseline parameterization, measurement reliability, and applied statistics."
pagetitle: "Research & publications — Ruwan C. Karunanayaka"
description: "Publications and research in experimental design, generalizability theory, measurement reliability, sports analytics, and the reliability of AI evaluation."
---

::: {.eyebrow}
Peer-reviewed
:::

## Publications

- **Bias reduction via complementation of orthogonal arrays under a baseline parameterization.**
  *Statistics & Probability Letters* **237** (2026), art. 110819.
  doi: [10.1016/j.spl.2026.110819](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.spl.2026.110819)

- **Is it time for a more analytical approach? Suboptimal shot selection strategies in handball.**
  (with A. G. Laxdal, S. Þorgeirsson, J. Saavedra, T. Haugen, and coauthors)
  *International Journal of Sports Science & Coaching* (2025).
  doi: [10.1177/17479541251369049](https://doi.org/10.1177/17479541251369049)

- **On the existence and constructions of orthogonal designs.**
  (with Boxin Tang)
  *Australian & New Zealand Journal of Statistics* (2018).

- **Compromise designs under baseline parametrization.**
  (with Boxin Tang)
  *Journal of Statistical Planning and Inference* (2017).

::: {.figure-caption}
The 2017 and 2018 design papers have since been cited in applied work across textile engineering, food science, hydrology, and mining.
:::

::: {.eyebrow}
Under review
:::

## Manuscripts under review

- **Distributional Generalizability Theory: Reliability for Non-Gaussian Measurements.**
  Under review, *Psychometrika* (the journal of the Psychometric Society).

- **Decomposing the uncertainty of AI fairness audits: a multivariate generalizability framework.**
  Under review, *RSS: Data Science and Artificial Intelligence* (Royal Statistical Society).

- **Response-scale reliability for count measurements: distributional generalizability theory for hurdle models, with an application to Twenty20 cricket.**
  Under review, *Australian & New Zealand Journal of Statistics* (ANZJS).

- **How Many Matches Before a Shooting Indicator Is Trustworthy? Generalizability of Team Shooting Performance in Elite Handball.**
  Under review, *International Journal of Sports Science & Coaching* (IJSSC).

- **Quantifying the Carleton Bias: A Multi-Facet Bayesian Account of Plate-Appearance Reliability.**
  Under review, *International Journal of Data Science and Analytics* (IJDSA).

- **Minimum aberration designs for random-effect generalizability studies.**
  Under review, *Statistical Papers*.

- **Election-Level Populism Scoring with Large Language Models: Coverage, Calibration, and Reliability.**
  In preparation.

- **Dynamic generalizability of expected goals (xG) in international soccer tournaments.**
  (with Tim Swartz)
  In preparation.

::: {.eyebrow}
Non-refereed
:::

## Conference contributions

- Walpitage D.L., Pathirage, D.P.N.A., Chamara, K.T.R., Pitigala, S.P. & Silva D.G.C.D. (2014). Investigating the reliability of a batting ability measurement system for T20 cricket: An application of random effects ANOVA through generalizability theory. *Proceedings of International Statistics Conference 2014*, Institute of Applied Statistics, Sri Lanka, 213.

- Walpitage, D.L., Pathirage, D.P.N.A., Dharmawardena, J.S.N.P., Chamara, K.T.R., Mohomad, M.H., Silva, D.G.C.D., Narasinghe, N.L.T.D. (2013, September). Standard ECG: Short dramas to introduce abstract statistical concepts. Paper presented for the European Network for Business and Industrial Statistics (ENBIS) Greenfield Challenge Award, 13th Annual Conference of ENBIS, Ankara, Turkey.

::: {.eyebrow}
Ongoing work
:::

## Active program

Beyond the manuscripts above, I maintain an active research program in measurement reliability, experimental design, and sports analytics — including the reliability of AI and machine-learning evaluation — with further work at various stages. The interactive tools the program produces are on the [projects page](projects.qmd).

::: {.eyebrow}
Service
:::

## Peer review

Reviewer, *Journal of Sports Analytics* (2024).

::: {.eyebrow}
Software
:::

## Code & tools

- **`dgt`** — R package for generalizability-theory decision studies. [github.com/rckaruna/dgt](https://github.com/rckaruna/dgt)
- **`StatcastG`** — Julia pipeline for multi-facet reliability analysis of MLB Statcast plate-appearance data. [github.com/rckaruna/StatcastG](https://github.com/rckaruna/StatcastG)
- **`fairness-gtheory`** — R code for reliable fairness auditing via multivariate generalizability theory. [github.com/rckaruna/fairness-gtheory](https://github.com/rckaruna/fairness-gtheory)
- **`MARE-code`** — companion R code for minimum-aberration random-effect designs (MARE). [github.com/rckaruna/MARE-code](https://github.com/rckaruna/MARE-code)
- **Forecasting applications** — deployed interactive tools for cricket, tennis, and soccer. [See projects](projects.qmd)
